hero.question: Will the US confirm that aliens exist before 2027?
hero.prob_take:
At 17.5%, the crowd is pricing alien confirmation as a 1-in-6 shot by 2027, which means they're betting against any major disclosure despite 3 years of congressional UAP hearings and Pentagon admissions that we can't explain what we're seeing.

The real question isn't whether aliens exist, it's whether the US government will formally confirm it, and that bar is absurdly high (think signed statement from POTUS or joint resolution), so shorts are probably right to keep this depressed, but a single credible leaked classified document could send this to 40% overnight.

daily_take.headline: Alien Confirmation Odds Jump 100bps. Market's Still Sleeping on UFO Disclosure Momentum.
daily_take.deck: The alien existence question just crept from 16.5% to 17.5% on $18.4M volume, a quiet but steady climb that suggests informed money is moving in. The smart read: Congress is getting serious about UAP transparency, government agencies are leaking faster than ever, and the 2027 window captures a real policy inflection point. At 17.5%, you're getting paid 5.7-to-1 odds on a binary that's probably worth 22-25% given the trajectory of disclosure. This one's still inefficient.
